page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6455B processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on the x86_64 architecture. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and single-core performance. The server achieves a top-tier rating in Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2278.0, alongside strong multi-core performance of 19792.0. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, reaching 4250.25 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 244.1 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database operations, the instance registers a strong PassMark database score of 26029.38 and processes 65.52 million Redis SET operations per second. For computational workloads, it demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.12xlarge offers high resource density by pairing high-frequency Intel Xeon processors with fast DDR5 memory, though the absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage for persistent data. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads that also require high CPU clock speeds, such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of fast 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core and memory performance. Benchmarks show top-tier results in Geekbench single-core execution and image processing, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of up to 4250.25 GB/sec. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on a 25 Gbps baseline network connection. It is designed for workloads requiring a balance of high-frequency compute and dense memory, such as database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core and strong database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-frequency memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding database and computational workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processor
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance and strong memory bandwidth benchmarks
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
